title: NOC 52120 – Graphic Designers and Illustrators | GTR Immigration
description: Creative professionals under NOC 52120 (Graphic designers and illustrators) are essential to Canada’s marketing, technology, and entertainment sectors. As a TEER 2 occupation, graphic designers are highly mobile and frequently qualify for immigration through tech-focused provincial draws and the Federal Skilled Worker program. At GTR Immigration, we help visual artists translate their portfolios into successful Permanent Residency applications.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category5 – Occupations in art, culture, recreation and sportTEER Level2Major Group52 – Technical occupations in art, culture and sportSub-Major Group521 – Technical occupations in art and cultureMinor Group5212 – Design occupationsNOC Code52120

duties: Consult with clients to establish the nature and content of illustrations or designs.
Develop and produce realistic or representational sketches and final illustrations.
Prepare specifications for design, including layout, typography, and color.
Use existing photo and illustration banks and typography guides.
Work in a multidisciplinary environment with other creative professionals.
employment_requirements: A university degree in visual arts with specialization in graphic design or a college diploma in graphic design is required.
Creative ability and artistic talent, as demonstrated by a portfolio of work, are required.
job_outlook: The outlook is Good. The integration of digital media in all business sectors ensures that graphic designers will remain in high demand. Over 20,000 new job openings are projected nationally over the next decade.
labour_market_demand: The demand for graphic designers is driven by the digital economy. From UI/UX design in Toronto’s tech sector to motion graphics in Vancouver’s film industry, the need for skilled visual communicators is high. Employers are increasingly looking for designers with multidisciplinary skills (video, web, and print).
Tech-Art Intersection: Massive growth in game design and tech start-ups.
Competitive Salaries: Median wages are strong, especially in urban centers.
PR Eligibility: Wide range of federal and provincial pathways.
pathways: Express Entry (FSW): A popular route for high-scoring candidates.
BC PNP Tech: Often includes designers related to the tech/film industries.
OINP Digital Technology Draw: Occasional draws targeting creative tech roles.
